import {
  ColorScales,
  TypographySamples,
  RadiusSamples,
} from "./theme-demo";

export const metadata = { title: "Theme" };

# Theme & Design Tokens

A single stylesheet (`tokens.css`) defines every color, size, and motion value in Patch UI. Components read from it through Tailwind utilities. Light and dark switch from one `.dark` class on `<html>`. No JavaScript required.

The scheme is deliberately small: a pure-neutral gray scale (light base `#FFFFFF`, dark base `#0A0A0A`), two surface families (`--layer-*` for lifted panels, `--fill-*` for tinted chips), four ink tiers, three hairline steps, three fixed semantic status roles, one monochrome primary. Consumers layer their own brand accent by redefining `--primary` in a single downstream layer.

## Colors

<ColorScales />

**Base + layers.** The page sits on `--base` — pure white in light, deep near-black `#0A0A0A` in dark. `--layer-1` is the default lifted panel: Card, Modal, Menu popup, Combobox popup, Tooltip, Select popup, Input, Table container, secondary Button chrome. In light `--layer-1` is *also* `#FFFFFF` — the hairline border alone signals the "lifted panel" state. In dark it steps ~3-5% brighter than base; the hairline sits under the card body rather than competing with it. `--layer-2` is a subtle hover step (secondary Button hover, Input hover companion).

**Fills.** `--fill-1` and `--fill-2` are tinted chip fills: Toggle track, Badge neutral, code chip, Switch off-track, skeleton block, striped table hover, active pill Tab, menu item highlight. In light they step deeper than base; in dark they step brighter than base. `--fill-2` sits distinctly above `--layer-1` in both themes so hovers, striped rows, and chips remain visible on top of Card / Popup surfaces.

**Interaction-state overlays.** `--layer-hover` and `--layer-selected` are alpha overlays (black in light, white in dark) that any transparent-at-rest control reaches for on `hover:`, `focus-visible:`, `data-[popup-open]:`, `data-[pressed]:`, and `active:`. Because they darken whatever surface sits underneath rather than paint over it, the state reads on base, `--layer-1`, `--fill-1`, or a consumer's own background without disappearing. `--layer-selected` is ~2x the hover strength so pressed always reads visibly darker than hover.

**Ink (text).** Four tiers, pure gray, no chroma. `--ink` is primary text (headings, body). `--ink-muted` is meta text and default icon tone. `--ink-subtle` is placeholder and "missing value" copy. `--ink-tertiary` is disabled and footnote.

**Semantic status.** `--error`, `--warning`, `--success` are fixed hex, identical in both themes so a warning always reads as a warning. Each has `-hover`, `-active`, and `-fg` variants; the `-fg` token carries label color so fill and text invert together.

**Hairlines.** Three tiers of solid hex, tuned to sit under the card body rather than competing with it. Light: `--hairline` `#EBEBEB`, `--hairline-strong` `#D4D4D4`, `--hairline-tertiary` `#A3A3A3`. Dark: `--hairline` `#262626`, `--hairline-strong` `#333333`, `--hairline-tertiary` `#454545`. Consumers who override `--base` should also override the hairlines to keep the border-vs-base contrast intact.

**Primary (monochrome).** `--primary` resolves to `--ink` and `--on-primary` to `--base`. No chromatic brand ships. `--primary-hover` and `--primary-active` are computed via `color-mix` toward `--base`, so a consumer's brand override on `--primary` automatically produces matching hover/active steps.

## Typography

Two families cover every use: **Inter** (variable) for everything that isn't code, **JetBrains Mono** for code. Size and weight are separate axes rather than a compound recipe.

**Size.** Eight tokens: `text-micro` (11), `text-mini` (12), `text-small` (13), `text-regular` (15, default body), `text-large` (18), `text-title3` (20), `text-title2` (24), `text-title1` (36).

**Weight.** Five tokens exposed as Tailwind utilities: `font-light` (300), `font-normal` (450, default body), `font-medium` (500), `font-semibold` (600), `font-bold` (700). Body defaults to 450, headings default to 500, so most call sites don't need an explicit weight.

**Line height.** Set on the element, not on the size utility. Body inherits 1.5, `<p>` uses 1.7, `<h1>–<h6>` use 1.25. A size utility on a `<div>` picks up 1.5 from body; wrapping the same content in `<p>` opens it up to 1.7.

<TypographySamples />

Rules of thumb:
- Reach for a size + weight pair (`text-small font-medium`) rather than inventing new compound tokens.
- Never `text-[Npx]` inline. If a size is missing, add it to the scale.
- Never `font-mono` for decoration. Reserve for `code`, `pre`, `kbd`, install snippets, and hex values shown as code.

## Sizing and motion

**Radius.** Four values.

<RadiusSamples />

`radius-6` for controls (Button, Input, Select, Checkbox, Menu row). `radius-12` for surfaces (Card, Dialog, Menu popup). `radius-16` for marketing chrome (hero panels). `radius-full` for chips (Badge, Avatar, pill Button).

**Shadows.** Elevation is flat with 1px hairline borders. Shadows appear only on true overlays: `--shadow-card` (marketing cards), `--shadow-menu` (Menu / Combobox / Select popup), `--shadow-tooltip` (Tooltip), `--shadow-modal` (Dialog / Sheet / Toast).

**Motion.** One easing (`--ease-standard`), two durations: `--duration-state` (75ms, for hover/active transitions) and `--duration-overlay` (150ms, for open/close). Under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`, both collapse to `0ms` automatically.

**Focus.** A 2px outline offset 2px outside the element border, colored `--primary` so it inherits any consumer brand override. Delivered through the `focusRing` recipe. Inputs are the intentional exception: on focus their border shifts to `--primary` instead of stacking an outside ring, so the input's own edge carries the state.

## Customizing

Every token is a plain CSS variable. Override in your own CSS after importing the tokens and every component picks up the change at runtime, no rebuild required:

```css
/* app/globals.css */
@import "tailwindcss";
@import "./styles/patch-ui-tokens.css";

/* Ship a lavender brand: every primary Button, active tab, focus
   ring, and slider indicator picks it up automatically. */
:root {
  --primary: #8b5cf6;
  --on-primary: #ffffff;
}

.dark {
  --primary: #a78bfa;
  --on-primary: #0f0f10;
}
```

If you edit tokens that affect readable text, run `npm run check:contrast -w packages/react` to verify the [contrast contract](/docs/contrast) still holds.
